The best, do we have time for story? Yeah, yeah. So it's platform selling, but it's the most profound example as I've ever seen. So for a number of years we were doing seminars for dentist chiropractors, ultimately podiatrist veterinarians. And I did some of them in social feather room, but our main speaker fell by the name of Foster Hebert. So Foster did most of the seminars. And Foster, we started at about $3.99, we wound up at about $8.99 as a price point. But he was basically selling a training program, all audio. And so the close was everybody got an order form passed out to them. And then you fill out the order forms and bring them back in. And Foster would walk them through the order form on front of them. Now, I'm going to write my name here, you write your name. It's the only funny thing you ever said. I'm going to write my name here, you write your name, not my name. And I'm going to use my American Express card. You use yours, not mine. It's the only funny thing you ever said, which he didn't know was funny. So I forget what city we were in now, but it was early in this game. So I was out on the road with him. We were still making things work. And I forget where we were, but there were a good 20, maybe 30 docs in the room. And they're all seated at classroom style. And so Foster closes, finishes the close. He now walks to the back of the room and the docs are supposed to bring their forms back there and get their bag of stuff. And nobody moves. They don't get up and go to the bathroom. They don't do nothing. They're not filling out forms. They are sitting there. Like Kevin and Robert used to say, a tree full of owls. And I'm standing in the doorway, right? Because if there's like a stampede stampede, I would step in and help them dispense bags. And I'm watching this. And I mean, this is like a minute, which is like an hour. It's like, I'm standing back in the room thinking, this is not good. This goes for a good three minutes. And now Foster picks up a pile of the stuff. He walks very slowly and sort of royally to the front of the room, all the way to the guy on the end and puts it down in front of him. He now walks all the way to the back of the room. He picks up another pile of stuff, walks all the way back to the front of the room, goes over to the second person, puts it down in front of him. He's not saying anything. Nobody's moving. I'm standing in the back thinking, I don't know, bloodshed. I don't know what's going to happen here, right? I've never seen anybody do it in my entire life. He does this to every one of them. And they all now are watching him. Their heads are turning, watching him back, walk to the back of the room, walk back up towards the front of the room. How many were in the room? 20, 25. This takes a long time. And he's not running. I mean, this is 15 minutes elapsed of this without a sound in the room. He then comes back and climbs up on the table and sits there. That's it. And I'm, I don't know what's going to happen. About another three, four, five minutes go by and finally the first guy cracks and brings his form back, right? And then one after the other brings his form back and everything's fine. Now, I ask him afterwards, where'd you get that? And of course he said, what? I said, that thing I did. You know, he said, I did that? I didn't know I did that. I must have been in a trance. And I'll forget. Maybe he was. But hardly anybody would have whatever you want to call it, right? The discipline to let this happen, right? Most just stop all over it. You know? I mean, and most are afraid to leave the room. I mean, if I have three or four there, when we've gotten to this point, I'll deliberately, I haven't taken a bathroom break all day. I'm taking a bathroom break. I'm really saying talk amongst yourselves. It's okay by me. I'm not worried about it. You guys figure it out. You know, at Disney, when we were at that point and we were pressed for time and so forth, I got Tony, God, I can't believe it was last day. He went and got himself a Coke-Cola. In a timeshare clothes. I mean, unheard of. You're going to leave the husband and wife in this room alone, right? But they've done everything right. It is Disney. I mean, it's like he knows we're going to talk amongst ourselves or need to. I'll go get myself a Coke-Cola if you don't mind. I'll be back in a couple of minutes. And that's an impressive point. I mean, you're having the discipline to follow the system and the process and realize that something needs to happen and not being afraid of losing the sale. That's right. Yeah. And again, you don't need 365 fancies. No, it's good to have three or four that will work. Yeah. You can have three or four in your toolbox that work. Yeah. That can be, if one doesn't work, you could try the other one. If the other one is not throwing a burk at you.
